instincts.trigger: Building a fluent API wrapper around a third-party library
instincts.action: Create a builder class with a private reference to the wrapped object. Use static factory methods (load, fromFile) that assign private fields directly — no as any. Expose public typed methods that delegate. Never leak the wrapped type.

instincts.trigger: Adding a composable cell style system
instincts.action: Use a style() function that deep-merges font/alignment/border and replaces fill. Create preset partials (font, fill, numFmt, border.thinBlack) as standalone exports. Map to excelts format via toExcelFont/etc. helpers.

instincts.trigger: Writing tests for a library wrapping excelts
instincts.action: Prefer real excelts objects over mocks. Assert on file output or inspect objects via subtype narrowing — never as any.

instincts.trigger: Designing overloaded method returning this or sub-builder for chaining
instincts.action: Use two overload signatures: (opts, configure: fn) => this and (opts) => SubBuilder. Implementation returns this when configure is provided.

instincts.trigger: Passing across a type boundary where library types are incompatible
instincts.action: Write a bridge function with an explicit return type that maps each field. Use type guards for discrimination. Only use as at single-field boundary with a comment.

instincts.trigger: Narrowing excelts union types (WorksheetView, Fill, CellValue)
instincts.action: Use discriminated union narrowing via the state/type discriminant. Write type guards where assignment narrowing is not possible.
